_meta.source: Derived from monitoring-app-trust-benchmark corpus (14 apps verified, Steps 1-5)
_meta.confidence_note: android_package_name is ONLY populated where confirmed from an official Play Store listing during research. Everything marked null is intentionally left unconfirmed rather than guessed -- this project does not fabricate evidence. The heuristic engine (see heuristic_model.json) is the primary detection mechanism precisely because exact package names for these operators change frequently (see 'known_reskin_operators').

known_reskin_operators.source: NY AG assurance of discontinuance, ag.ny.gov

signatures.covert_operation_evidence: Marketing states 'totally hidden' / 'completely confidential', no icon visible on target device.
signatures.enforcement_record: 2015 data breach reported (Krebs on Security), disputed by mSpy.

signatures.covert_operation_evidence: Marketed as '100% hidden', requires root/jailbreak, own ToS acknowledges illegality if installed without consent.
FAQ: 'Secretly track and spy on virtually any cell phone... completely undetected.'
signatures.enforcement_record: NY AG assurance of discontinuance vs. ILF Mobile Apps Corp -- also operates Auto Forward, Easy Spy, DDI Utilities, PhoneSpector under the same entity.

signatures.covert_operation_evidence: FAQ: 'XNSPY mobile monitoring app is 100% invisible and stays hidden... will not show in the installed apps list.'

signatures.package_name_source: Confirmed via Google Play Store listing during corpus verification.
signatures.sanity_flag: Do not confuse with eyezie.com, an unrelated e-commerce site.

Operator's own Acceptable Use Policy PROHIBITS adult/partner monitoring even with consent, yet its own marketing pages market complete invisibility -- an internal self-contradiction, not just a marketing/reality gap.
signatures.covert_operation_evidence: Marketing page: 'stays completely hidden -- no icons or alerts give it away.'
Features page: 'runs in stealth mode, which makes it impossible for kids to tamper.'
Marketing: 'app is invisible to the end user, so no one will know you installed it.'

signatures.sanity_flag: Name collision with an UNRELATED iOS app 'SpyNger: Find Family & Friends' by developer bouzkriapps -- do not conflate signatures.
signatures.covert_operation_evidence: Own FAQ: 'Cover your tracks... your partner won't even know the app is installed.'

signatures.note: BASELINE (non-covert). Child is informed during setup; AI-alert model, not raw message dump. Included for the detector to correctly NOT flag this as stalkerware.
BASELINE (non-covert). Own help center confirms: cannot hide on mobile devices due to technical limitations.
BASELINE (non-covert). Own blog: 'Can You Track Someone on Life360 Without Them Knowing? ... No.'
signatures.sanity_flag: Do not confuse with life360.bio, an unrelated biologics company.

signatures.note: No install on target device -- NOT detectable by an on-device app scanner. Requires target to click a link. Out of scope for this Android tool; relevant to a future browser-extension companion tool instead.
signatures.sanity_flag: Domain mismatch: marketed at scannero.io, privacy policy hosted at scannero.top under a different registered entity (Hong Kong Reverse Technology Limited). Low third-party trust scores.
